item.title: U.S. missile burn rate in Iran leaves the Pacific cupboard bare
item.description: China is paying attention as America depletes its stockpile.

item.title: This poll shows the political risk to Israel from the war in Iran
item.description: Launching the attack was a big gamble with American opinion that isn’t paying off.

item.title: Trump’s outrageous Hormuz talk shuns basic strategy
item.description: U.S. power rests on open seas.

item.title: Why Catholic converts are surging with an unexpected demographic
item.description: For a growing segment of young people, religion is rebellion.

item.title: An unpardonable abuse of presidential power with only one solution
item.description: The “power to grant reprieves and pardons” has become another source of political brutishness.

item.title: U.S. water infrastructure is vulnerable to hacks. Iran knows it.
item.description: Many water facilities lack basic cybersecurity measures, making them vulnerable to adversaries.

item.title: I'm not anti-tax. But this one should go.
item.description: Host Megan McArdle breaks down why the corporate tax system is so complex, costly and potentially inefficient — and explores a bold idea: What if we eliminated it altogether? From hidden economic trade-offs to who really pays corporate taxes, this episode challenges how we think about fairness, efficiency and the future of taxation.

item.title: Rerun
item.description: Michael Ramirez cartoon on Trump, Iran war, ceasefire.

item.title: NATO’s deeper problem
item.description: Deficit-financed militaries are not sustainable. It’s time for Europe to talk trade-offs.

item.title: Never subsidize a sports stadium
item.description:
An upcoming Washington Post Opinions show. Stay tuned.
00:00 - Monologue 02:22 - Never subsidize a sports stadium 13:53 - Is the Iran ceasefire over already? 25:39 - VA spending spiked. Are veterans better off?

item.title: New Jersey chips away at irrational anti-nuclear policies
item.description: Mikie Sherrill is the second governor this year to reverse a nuclear power moratorium.

item.title: Meet Abi, the AI-powered robot companion for senior care
item.description: This new tech from Australia is coming to America’s senior care facilities.

item.title: Students are asking the wrong questions about advanced degrees
item.description: Plus: How parents can opt out of school screen time

item.title: What Trump might see from space
item.description: The Artemis II astronauts described a view that could clearly benefit the president.

item.title: How Trump could still pull out a win in Iran
item.description: An end to the war would hasten the regime’s decay.

item.title: Shooting $100 billion in taxpayer funds to the moon is not a win
item.description: The private economy is winning the space race, not NASA.

item.title: The director who could have been Spielberg chose something better
item.description: The Steven Soderbergh story: 37 films. One short retirement. Zero compromises.

item.title: This is a tale of two outbreaks. The difference is RFK Jr.
item.description: These raw dairy E. coli outbreaks are exposing something troubling.

item.title: The Iran ceasefire pushes the Gulf toward what it fears most
item.description: A pause that preserves Tehran’s capacity for coercion will destroy the region.

item.title: Countless Americans face a scary retirement. This rule could change that.
item.description: When it comes to retirement investing, the deck is stacked against most workers.

item.title: The Iran war’s exit ramp has become a diplomatic maze
item.description: The war began without a strategy for victory. The peace has no formula either.

item.title: How to handle Iran talks
item.description: Tehran has more to lose from the war resuming than Trump.

item.title: Iran thinks it has leverage. Here’s how Trump can prove it wrong.
item.description: If Iran won’t accept the president’s terms in two weeks, he should impose them.

item.title: The anti-data center movement is growing — and becoming more unhinged
item.description: As Wisconsin and Maine move against AI, an Indianapolis councilor experiences an act of violence.

item.title: The Iran ceasefire was a TACO Tuesday, and thank goodness
item.description: Trump gets to act like his bloodcurdling threats worked, but he’s giving up far more than Tehran did.

item.title: A bipartisan bill that would hurt employers and unions
item.description: The Faster Labor Contracts Act would let arbitrators force bad agreements on workers and businesses.

item.title: Biggest TACO yet? Trump backtracks from 'Living Hell' threat
item.description:
An upcoming Washington Post Opinions show. Stay tuned.
00:00 - Monologue 02:33 - Biggest TACO yet? Trump backtracks from 'Living Hell' threat 19:26 - Annie Ramos detained while securing military spouse benefits 29:04 - Jamie Dimon warns businesses may leave under Mamdani tax policy

item.title: Sen. Edward J. Markey: ‘Liberation Day’ was ‘Obliteration Day’ for small businesses
item.description: Readers on tariffs, Trump’s Iran language and more.

item.title: Trump controls Republicans with fear. Now comes a test.
item.description: The outcome of the president’s beef with five state senators in Indiana could reshape his second term.
